[[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] | main_branches = Yafu Clan ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}})
Shiramatsu Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Hara Clan|ja|原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hara Clan|1=Hara Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975252}} (Samurai)
Yanagi Clan (Samurai)
Ue Clan (Samurai)
Akiyoshi Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Kimura Clan|ja|木村氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimura Clan|1=Kimura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518978}} (Samurai)
Tominaga Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Minami Clan|ja|南氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Clan|1=Minami Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408034}} (Samurai) {{Sfn|Misono|1959|loc=§10 Local Lords of Nagato and Suō}} }} The '''Kotō Clan''' (厚東氏) was one of the {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, centered in [[:en:Nagato Province|Nagato Province]] as local magnates and eventually serving as provincial [[:en:Shugo|shugo]] (military governors). The clan’s {{ill|honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}} (original family name) was [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe]], and it claimed descent from [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]]. The Akiyoshi Clan, a branch family, traces its roots to Takeuji, the third son of the 11th head of the Kotō Clan. == Overview == According to the "Kotō Genealogy" (厚東氏系図), the clan originated from Mononobe no Moriya. It is said that his descendant Taketada established a residence in Tanai (modern-day [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube, Yamaguchi]], Yamaguchi Prefecture). During the Heian period, a figure named Mononobe Sukune Motoyo served as governor of Nagato, indicating the clan's early establishment in the region. Initially, the Kotō Clan governed the eastern part of Asa District as local warriors but expanded their influence over time. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, the clan initially supported the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira Clan]] and participated in the [[:en:Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|Battle of Ichi-no-Tani]]. However, they later sided with the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto Clan]]. Their contributions at the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] earned them recognition, granting them dominion over the Asa District. The clan also participated in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (1274 and 1281) alongside the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi Clan]] under the orders of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]. == Decline and Demise == The Kotō Clan’s decline began during its conflict with the Ōuchi Clan. After losing critical battles, including the {{ill|Battle of Shimo-furiyama|ja|霜降城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Shimo-furiyama|1=Battle of Shimo-furiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660792}}, Kotō Yoshitake (義武) retreated to his holdings in {{ill|Kikuchi District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}, Buzen Province. Although a temporary truce with the Ōuchi Clan allowed the Kotō Clan to reclaim Nagato, their political power diminished. By 1362, the {{ill|Muromachi Shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} formally assigned the role of Nagato’s shugo to Ōuchi Hiroyo, marking the Kotō Clan’s effective downfall. == List of Family Heads == 1. {{ill|Kotō Taketada|ja|厚東武忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Taketada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2. {{ill|Kotō Takemoto|ja|厚東武基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Takemoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ... 17. {{ill|Kotō Yoshitake|ja|厚東義武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Yoshitake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} * [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] * [[:en:Asa District, Yamaguchi|Asa District]] == References == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|date=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|url={{NDLDC|1130938/225}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Fujii|first=Takashi|date=2013|title=Muromachi Period Daimyō Power Structures|publisher=Dōseisha}} {{translated page|ja|厚東氏|version=101563902|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] }} [[ja:厚東氏]] [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Koto Clan]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] {{wikidata link|Q11409278}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{ill|Kimura Clan|ja|木村氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimura Clan|1=Kimura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518978}} (Samurai)
Tominaga Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Minami Clan|ja|南氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Clan|1=Minami Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408034}} (Samurai) {{Sfn|Misono|1959|loc=§10 Local Lords of Nagato and Suō}} }} The '''Kotō Clan''' (厚東氏) was one of the {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, centered in [[:en:Nagato Province|Nagato Province]] as local magnates and eventually serving as provincial [[:en:Shugo|shugo]] (military governors). The clan’s {{ill|honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}} (original family name) was [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe]], and it claimed descent from [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]]. The Akiyoshi Clan, a branch family, traces its roots to Takeuji, the third son of the 11th head of the Kotō Clan. == Overview == According to the "Kotō Genealogy" (厚東氏系図), the clan originated from Mononobe no Moriya. It is said that his descendant Taketada established a residence in Tanai (modern-day [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube, Yamaguchi]], Yamaguchi Prefecture). During the Heian period, a figure named Mononobe Sukune Motoyo served as governor of Nagato, indicating the clan's early establishment in the region. Initially, the Kotō Clan governed the eastern part of Asa District as local warriors but expanded their influence over time. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, the clan initially supported the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira Clan]] and participated in the [[:en:Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|Battle of Ichi-no-Tani]]. However, they later sided with the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto Clan]]. Their contributions at the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] earned them recognition, granting them dominion over the Asa District. The clan also participated in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (1274 and 1281) alongside the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi Clan]] under the orders of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]. == Decline and Demise == The Kotō Clan’s decline began during its conflict with the Ōuchi Clan. After losing critical battles, including the {{ill|Battle of Shimo-furiyama|ja|霜降城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Shimo-furiyama|1=Battle of Shimo-furiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660792}}, Kotō Yoshitake (義武) retreated to his holdings in {{ill|Kikuchi District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}, Buzen Province. Although a temporary truce with the Ōuchi Clan allowed the Kotō Clan to reclaim Nagato, their political power diminished. By 1362, the {{ill|Muromachi Shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} formally assigned the role of Nagato’s shugo to Ōuchi Hiroyo, marking the Kotō Clan’s effective downfall. == List of Family Heads == 1. {{ill|Kotō Taketada|ja|厚東武忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Taketada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2. {{ill|Kotō Takemoto|ja|厚東武基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Takemoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ... 17. {{ill|Kotō Yoshitake|ja|厚東義武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Yoshitake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} * [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] * [[:en:Asa District, Yamaguchi|Asa District]] == References == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|date=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|url={{NDLDC|1130938/225}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Fujii|first=Takashi|date=2013|title=Muromachi Period Daimyō Power Structures|publisher=Dōseisha}} {{translated page|ja|厚東氏|version=101563902|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] }} [[ja:厚東氏]] [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Koto Clan]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] {{wikidata link|Q11409278}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

By 1362, the {{ill|Muromachi Shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} formally assigned the role of Nagato’s shugo to Ōuchi Hiroyo, marking the Kotō Clan’s effective downfall. == List of Family Heads == 1. {{ill|Kotō Taketada|ja|厚東武忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Taketada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2. {{ill|Kotō Takemoto|ja|厚東武基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Takemoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ... 17. {{ill|Kotō Yoshitake|ja|厚東義武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Yoshitake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} * [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] * [[:en:Asa District, Yamaguchi|Asa District]] == References == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|date=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|url={{NDLDC|1130938/225}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Fujii|first=Takashi|date=2013|title=Muromachi Period Daimyō Power Structures|publisher=Dōseisha}} {{translated page|ja|厚東氏|version=101563902|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] }} [[ja:厚東氏]] [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Koto Clan]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] {{wikidata link|Q11409278}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:厚東氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11409278]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11409278]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kotō Clan | crest = | crest_caption = Rin-no-chigai | home_province = [[:en:Nagato Province|Nagato Province]], Eastern [[:en:Asa District, Yamaguchi|Asa District]] {{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=2263}} | headquarters = Eastern Asa District, Nagato Province
{{ill|Shimofuriyama Castle|ja|霜降山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimofuriyama Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=2264}}
{{ill|Kikuchi District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}, Buzen Province
[[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] | main_branches = Yafu Clan ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}})
Shiramatsu Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Hara Clan|ja|原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hara Clan|1=Hara Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975252}} (Samurai)
Yanagi Clan (Samurai)
Ue Clan (Samurai)
Akiyoshi Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Kimura Clan|ja|木村氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimura Clan|1=Kimura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518978}} (Samurai)
Tominaga Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Minami Clan|ja|南氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Clan|1=Minami Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408034}} (Samurai) {{Sfn|Misono|1959|loc=§10 Local Lords of Nagato and Suō}} }} The '''Kotō Clan''' (厚東氏) was one of the {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, centered in [[:en:Nagato Province|Nagato Province]] as local magnates and eventually serving as provincial [[:en:Shugo|shugo]] (military governors). The clan’s {{ill|honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}} (original family name) was [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe]], and it claimed descent from [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]]. The Akiyoshi Clan, a branch family, traces its roots to Takeuji, the third son of the 11th head of the Kotō Clan. == Overview == According to the "Kotō Genealogy" (厚東氏系図), the clan originated from Mononobe no Moriya. It is said that his descendant Taketada established a residence in Tanai (modern-day [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube, Yamaguchi]], Yamaguchi Prefecture). During the Heian period, a figure named Mononobe Sukune Motoyo served as governor of Nagato, indicating the clan's early establishment in the region. Initially, the Kotō Clan governed the eastern part of Asa District as local warriors but expanded their influence over time. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, the clan initially supported the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira Clan]] and participated in the [[:en:Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|Battle of Ichi-no-Tani]]. However, they later sided with the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto Clan]]. Their contributions at the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] earned them recognition, granting them dominion over the Asa District. The clan also participated in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (1274 and 1281) alongside the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi Clan]] under the orders of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]. == Decline and Demise == The Kotō Clan’s decline began during its conflict with the Ōuchi Clan. After losing critical battles, including the {{ill|Battle of Shimo-furiyama|ja|霜降城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Shimo-furiyama|1=Battle of Shimo-furiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660792}}, Kotō Yoshitake (義武) retreated to his holdings in {{ill|Kikuchi District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}, Buzen Province. Although a temporary truce with the Ōuchi Clan allowed the Kotō Clan to reclaim Nagato, their political power diminished. By 1362, the {{ill|Muromachi Shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} formally assigned the role of Nagato’s shugo to Ōuchi Hiroyo, marking the Kotō Clan’s effective downfall. == List of Family Heads == 1. {{ill|Kotō Taketada|ja|厚東武忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Taketada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2. {{ill|Kotō Takemoto|ja|厚東武基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Takemoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ... 17. {{ill|Kotō Yoshitake|ja|厚東義武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Yoshitake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} * [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] * [[:en:Asa District, Yamaguchi|Asa District]] == References == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|date=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|url={{NDLDC|1130938/225}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Fujii|first=Takashi|date=2013|title=Muromachi Period Daimyō Power Structures|publisher=Dōseisha}} {{translated page|ja|厚東氏|version=101563902|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] }} [[ja:厚東氏]] [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Koto Clan]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] {{wikidata link|Q11409278}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|Kimura Clan|ja|木村氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimura Clan|1=Kimura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518978}} (Samurai)
Tominaga Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Minami Clan|ja|南氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Clan|1=Minami Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408034}} (Samurai) {{Sfn|Misono|1959|loc=§10 Local Lords of Nagato and Suō}} }} The '''Kotō Clan''' (厚東氏) was one of the {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, centered in [[:en:Nagato Province|Nagato Province]] as local magnates and eventually serving as provincial [[:en:Shugo|shugo]] (military governors). The clan’s {{ill|honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}} (original family name) was [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe]], and it claimed descent from [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]]. The Akiyoshi Clan, a branch family, traces its roots to Takeuji, the third son of the 11th head of the Kotō Clan. == Overview == According to the "Kotō Genealogy" (厚東氏系図), the clan originated from Mononobe no Moriya. It is said that his descendant Taketada established a residence in Tanai (modern-day [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube, Yamaguchi]], Yamaguchi Prefecture). During the Heian period, a figure named Mononobe Sukune Motoyo served as governor of Nagato, indicating the clan's early establishment in the region. Initially, the Kotō Clan governed the eastern part of Asa District as local warriors but expanded their influence over time. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, the clan initially supported the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira Clan]] and participated in the [[:en:Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|Battle of Ichi-no-Tani]]. However, they later sided with the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto Clan]]. Their contributions at the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] earned them recognition, granting them dominion over the Asa District. The clan also participated in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (1274 and 1281) alongside the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi Clan]] under the orders of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]. == Decline and Demise == The Kotō Clan’s decline began during its conflict with the Ōuchi Clan. After losing critical battles, including the {{ill|Battle of Shimo-furiyama|ja|霜降城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Shimo-furiyama|1=Battle of Shimo-furiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660792}}, Kotō Yoshitake (義武) retreated to his holdings in {{ill|Kikuchi District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}, Buzen Province. Although a temporary truce with the Ōuchi Clan allowed the Kotō Clan to reclaim Nagato, their political power diminished. By 1362, the {{ill|Muromachi Shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} formally assigned the role of Nagato’s shugo to Ōuchi Hiroyo, marking the Kotō Clan’s effective downfall. == List of Family Heads == 1. {{ill|Kotō Taketada|ja|厚東武忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Taketada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2. {{ill|Kotō Takemoto|ja|厚東武基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Takemoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ... 17. {{ill|Kotō Yoshitake|ja|厚東義武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Yoshitake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} * [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] * [[:en:Asa District, Yamaguchi|Asa District]] == References == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|date=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|url={{NDLDC|1130938/225}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Fujii|first=Takashi|date=2013|title=Muromachi Period Daimyō Power Structures|publisher=Dōseisha}} {{translated page|ja|厚東氏|version=101563902|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:厚東氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11409278}} [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Koto Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

By 1362, the {{ill|Muromachi Shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} formally assigned the role of Nagato’s shugo to Ōuchi Hiroyo, marking the Kotō Clan’s effective downfall. == List of Family Heads == 1. {{ill|Kotō Taketada|ja|厚東武忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Taketada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2. {{ill|Kotō Takemoto|ja|厚東武基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Takemoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ... 17. {{ill|Kotō Yoshitake|ja|厚東義武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Yoshitake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} * [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] * [[:en:Asa District, Yamaguchi|Asa District]] == References == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|date=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|url={{NDLDC|1130938/225}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Fujii|first=Takashi|date=2013|title=Muromachi Period Daimyō Power Structures|publisher=Dōseisha}} {{translated page|ja|厚東氏|version=101563902|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:厚東氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11409278}} [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Koto Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:厚東氏]] [[ja:厚東氏]] [[ja:厚東氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11409278]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11409278]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kotō Clan | crest = | crest_caption = Rin-no-chigai | home_province = [[:en:Nagato Province|Nagato Province]], Eastern [[:en:Asa District, Yamaguchi|Asa District]] {{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=2263}} | headquarters = Eastern Asa District, Nagato Province
{{ill|Shimofuriyama Castle|ja|霜降山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimofuriyama Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=2264}}
{{ill|Kikuchi District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}, Buzen Province
[[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] | main_branches = Yafu Clan ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}})
Shiramatsu Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Hara Clan|ja|原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hara Clan|1=Hara Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975252}} (Samurai)
Yanagi Clan (Samurai)
Ue Clan (Samurai)
Akiyoshi Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Kimura Clan|ja|木村氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimura Clan|1=Kimura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518978}} (Samurai)
Tominaga Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Minami Clan|ja|南氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Clan|1=Minami Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408034}} (Samurai) {{Sfn|Misono|1959|loc=§10 Local Lords of Nagato and Suō}} }} The '''Kotō Clan''' (厚東氏) was one of the {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, centered in [[:en:Nagato Province|Nagato Province]] as local magnates and eventually serving as provincial [[:en:Shugo|shugo]] (military governors). The clan’s {{ill|honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}} (original family name) was [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe]], and it claimed descent from [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]]. The Akiyoshi Clan, a branch family, traces its roots to Takeuji, the third son of the 11th head of the Kotō Clan. == Overview == According to the "Kotō Genealogy" (厚東氏系図), the clan originated from Mononobe no Moriya. It is said that his descendant Taketada established a residence in Tanai (modern-day [[:en:Ube, Yamaguchi|Ube, Yamaguchi]], Yamaguchi Prefecture). During the Heian period, a figure named Mononobe Sukune Motoyo served as governor of Nagato, indicating the clan's early establishment in the region. Initially, the Kotō Clan governed the eastern part of Asa District as local warriors but expanded their influence over time. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, the clan initially supported the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira Clan]] and participated in the [[:en:Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|Battle of Ichi-no-Tani]]. However, they later sided with the [[:en:Minamoto clan|Minamoto Clan]]. Their contributions at the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] earned them recognition, granting them dominion over the Asa District. The clan also participated in the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]] (1274 and 1281) alongside the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi Clan]] under the orders of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]. == Decline and Demise == The Kotō Clan’s decline began during its conflict with the Ōuchi Clan. After losing critical battles, including the {{ill|Battle of Shimo-furiyama|ja|霜降城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Shimo-furiyama|1=Battle of Shimo-furiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660792}}, Kotō Yoshitake (義武) retreated to his holdings in {{ill|Kikuchi District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}, Buzen Province. Although a temporary truce with the Ōuchi Clan allowed the Kotō Clan to reclaim Nagato, their political power diminished. By 1362, the {{ill|Muromachi Shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} formally assigned the role of Nagato’s shugo to Ōuchi Hiroyo, marking the Kotō Clan’s effective downfall. == List of Family Heads == 1. {{ill|Kotō Taketada|ja|厚東武忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Taketada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2. {{ill|Kotō Takemoto|ja|厚東武基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Takemoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ... 17. {{ill|Kotō Yoshitake|ja|厚東義武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Yoshitake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} * [[:en:Shugo|Shugo]] * [[:en:Asa District, Yamaguchi|Asa District]] == References == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|date=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|url={{NDLDC|1130938/225}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Fujii|first=Takashi|date=2013|title=Muromachi Period Daimyō Power Structures|publisher=Dōseisha}} {{translated page|ja|厚東氏|version=101563902|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:厚東氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11409278}} [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Koto Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:厚東氏]] [[ja:厚東氏]] [[ja:厚東氏]] {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kotō Clan | crest = | crest_caption = Rin-no-chigai | home_province = {{ill|Nagato Province|en|Nagato Province|WD=Q907382|lt=Nagato Province}}, Eastern {{ill|Asa District, Yamaguchi|en|Asa District, Yamaguchi|WD=Q4803120|lt=Asa District}} {{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=2263}} | headquarters = Eastern Asa District, Nagato Province
{{ill|Shimofuriyama Castle|ja|霜降山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimofuriyama Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=2264}}
{{ill|Kikuchi District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}, Buzen Province
{{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province}} | main_branches = Yafu Clan ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}})
Shiramatsu Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Hara Clan|ja|原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hara Clan|1=Hara Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975252}} (Samurai)
Yanagi Clan (Samurai)
Ue Clan (Samurai)
Akiyoshi Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Kimura Clan|ja|木村氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimura Clan|1=Kimura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518978}} (Samurai)
Tominaga Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Minami Clan|ja|南氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Clan|1=Minami Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408034}} (Samurai) {{Sfn|Misono|1959|loc=§10 Local Lords of Nagato and Suō}} }} The '''Kotō Clan''' (厚東氏) was one of the {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, centered in {{ill|Nagato Province|en|Nagato Province|WD=Q907382|lt=Nagato Province}} as local magnates and eventually serving as provincial {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=shugo}} (military governors). The clan’s {{ill|honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}} (original family name) was {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe}}, and it claimed descent from {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}. The Akiyoshi Clan, a branch family, traces its roots to Takeuji, the third son of the 11th head of the Kotō Clan. == Overview == According to the "Kotō Genealogy" (厚東氏系図), the clan originated from Mononobe no Moriya. It is said that his descendant Taketada established a residence in Tanai (modern-day {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|en|Ube, Yamaguchi|WD=Q468765|lt=Ube, Yamaguchi}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture). During the Heian period, a figure named Mononobe Sukune Motoyo served as governor of Nagato, indicating the clan's early establishment in the region. Initially, the Kotō Clan governed the eastern part of Asa District as local warriors but expanded their influence over time. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, the clan initially supported the {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira Clan}} and participated in the {{ill|Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|en|Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|WD=Q2116658|lt=Battle of Ichi-no-Tani}}. However, they later sided with the {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Minamoto Clan}}. Their contributions at the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|WD=Q968676|lt=Battle of Dan-no-ura}} earned them recognition, granting them dominion over the Asa District. The clan also participated in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}} (1274 and 1281) alongside the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi Clan}} under the orders of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate}}. == Decline and Demise == The Kotō Clan’s decline began during its conflict with the Ōuchi Clan. After losing critical battles, including the {{ill|Battle of Shimo-furiyama|ja|霜降城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Shimo-furiyama|1=Battle of Shimo-furiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660792}}, Kotō Yoshitake (義武) retreated to his holdings in {{ill|Kikuchi District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}, Buzen Province. Although a temporary truce with the Ōuchi Clan allowed the Kotō Clan to reclaim Nagato, their political power diminished. By 1362, the {{ill|Muromachi Shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} formally assigned the role of Nagato’s shugo to Ōuchi Hiroyo, marking the Kotō Clan’s effective downfall. == List of Family Heads == 1. {{ill|Kotō Taketada|ja|厚東武忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Taketada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2. {{ill|Kotō Takemoto|ja|厚東武基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Takemoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ... 17. {{ill|Kotō Yoshitake|ja|厚東義武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Yoshitake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} * {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=Shugo}} * {{ill|Asa District, Yamaguchi|en|Asa District, Yamaguchi|WD=Q4803120|lt=Asa District}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|date=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|url={{NDLDC|1130938/225}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Fujii|first=Takashi|date=2013|title=Muromachi Period Daimyō Power Structures|publisher=Dōseisha}} {{translated page|ja|厚東氏|version=101563902|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:厚東氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11409278}} [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Koto Clan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

{{ill|Kikuchi District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}, Buzen Province
{{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province}} | main_branches = Yafu Clan ({{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}})
Shiramatsu Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Hara Clan|ja|原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hara Clan|1=Hara Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975252}} (Samurai)
Yanagi Clan (Samurai)
Ue Clan (Samurai)
Akiyoshi Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Kimura Clan|ja|木村氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimura Clan|1=Kimura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518978}} (Samurai)
Tominaga Clan (Samurai)
{{ill|Minami Clan|ja|南氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Clan|1=Minami Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408034}} (Samurai) {{Sfn|Misono|1959|loc=§10 Local Lords of Nagato and Suō}} }} The '''Kotō Clan''' (厚東氏) was one of the {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, centered in {{ill|Nagato Province|en|Nagato Province|WD=Q907382|lt=Nagato Province}} as local magnates and eventually serving as provincial {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=shugo}} (military governors). The clan’s {{ill|honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}} (original family name) was {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe}}, and it claimed descent from {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya}}. The Akiyoshi Clan, a branch family, traces its roots to Takeuji, the third son of the 11th head of the Kotō Clan. == Overview == According to the "Kotō Genealogy" (厚東氏系図), the clan originated from Mononobe no Moriya. It is said that his descendant Taketada established a residence in Tanai (modern-day {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|en|Ube, Yamaguchi|WD=Q468765|lt=Ube, Yamaguchi}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture). During the Heian period, a figure named Mononobe Sukune Motoyo served as governor of Nagato, indicating the clan's early establishment in the region. Initially, the Kotō Clan governed the eastern part of Asa District as local warriors but expanded their influence over time. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, the clan initially supported the {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira Clan}} and participated in the {{ill|Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|en|Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|WD=Q2116658|lt=Battle of Ichi-no-Tani}}. However, they later sided with the {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Minamoto Clan}}. Their contributions at the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|WD=Q968676|lt=Battle of Dan-no-ura}} earned them recognition, granting them dominion over the Asa District. The clan also participated in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}} (1274 and 1281) alongside the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi Clan}} under the orders of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate}}. == Decline and Demise == The Kotō Clan’s decline began during its conflict with the Ōuchi Clan. After losing critical battles, including the {{ill|Battle of Shimo-furiyama|ja|霜降城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Shimo-furiyama|1=Battle of Shimo-furiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660792}}, Kotō Yoshitake (義武) retreated to his holdings in {{ill|Kikuchi District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}, Buzen Province. Although a temporary truce with the Ōuchi Clan allowed the Kotō Clan to reclaim Nagato, their political power diminished. By 1362, the {{ill|Muromachi Shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} formally assigned the role of Nagato’s shugo to Ōuchi Hiroyo, marking the Kotō Clan’s effective downfall. == List of Family Heads == 1. {{ill|Kotō Taketada|ja|厚東武忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Taketada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2. {{ill|Kotō Takemoto|ja|厚東武基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Takemoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ... 17. {{ill|Kotō Yoshitake|ja|厚東義武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Yoshitake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} * {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=Shugo}} * {{ill|Asa District, Yamaguchi|en|Asa District, Yamaguchi|WD=Q4803120|lt=Asa District}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|date=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|url={{NDLDC|1130938/225}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Fujii|first=Takashi|date=2013|title=Muromachi Period Daimyō Power Structures|publisher=Dōseisha}} {{translated page|ja|厚東氏|version=101563902|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:厚東氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11409278}} [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Koto Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Minami Clan|ja|南氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Clan|1=Q11408034|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408034}} (Samurai) {{Sfn|Misono|1959|loc=§10 Local Lords of Nagato and Suō}} }} The '''Kotō Clan''' (厚東氏) was one of the {{ill|Japanese clans|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, centered in {{ill|Nagato Province|en|Nagato Province|WD=Q907382|lt=Nagato Province|1=Nagato Province}} as local magnates and eventually serving as provincial {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=shugo|1=Shugo}} (military governors). The clan’s {{ill|honsei|ja|本姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/honsei|1=honsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520116}} (original family name) was {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe|1=Mononobe clan}}, and it claimed descent from {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|en|Mononobe no Moriya|WD=Q2424736|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|1=Mononobe no Moriya}}. The Akiyoshi Clan, a branch family, traces its roots to Takeuji, the third son of the 11th head of the Kotō Clan. == Overview == According to the "Kotō Genealogy" (厚東氏系図), the clan originated from Mononobe no Moriya. It is said that his descendant Taketada established a residence in Tanai (modern-day {{ill|Ube, Yamaguchi|en|Ube, Yamaguchi|WD=Q468765|lt=Ube, Yamaguchi|1=Ube, Yamaguchi}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture). During the Heian period, a figure named Mononobe Sukune Motoyo served as governor of Nagato, indicating the clan's early establishment in the region. Initially, the Kotō Clan governed the eastern part of Asa District as local warriors but expanded their influence over time. During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|源平合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 治承・寿永の乱|1=Genpei War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q737542}}, the clan initially supported the {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira Clan|1=Taira clan}} and participated in the {{ill|Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|en|Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|WD=Q2116658|lt=Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|1=Battle of Ichi-no-Tani}}. However, they later sided with the {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|WD=Q599881|lt=Minamoto Clan|1=Minamoto clan}}. Their contributions at the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|WD=Q968676|lt=Battle of Dan-no-ura|1=Battle of Dan-no-ura}} earned them recognition, granting them dominion over the Asa District. The clan also participated in the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}} (1274 and 1281) alongside the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi Clan|1=Ōuchi clan}} under the orders of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}. == Decline and Demise == The Kotō Clan’s decline began during its conflict with the Ōuchi Clan. After losing critical battles, including the {{ill|Battle of Shimo-furiyama|ja|霜降城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Shimo-furiyama|1=Q11660792|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660792}}, Kotō Yoshitake (義武) retreated to his holdings in {{ill|Kikuchi District|ja|企救郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi District|1=Kiku district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884792}}, Buzen Province. Although a temporary truce with the Ōuchi Clan allowed the Kotō Clan to reclaim Nagato, their political power diminished. By 1362, the {{ill|Muromachi Shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1154406}} formally assigned the role of Nagato’s shugo to Ōuchi Hiroyo, marking the Kotō Clan’s effective downfall. == List of Family Heads == 1. {{ill|Kotō Taketada|ja|厚東武忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Taketada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2. {{ill|Kotō Takemoto|ja|厚東武基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Takemoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ... 17. {{ill|Kotō Yoshitake|ja|厚東義武|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotō Yoshitake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * {{ill|Samurai|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Buke|WD=Q2927809}} * {{ill|Shugo|en|Shugo|WD=Q1141023|lt=Shugo|1=Shugo}} * {{ill|Asa District, Yamaguchi|en|Asa District, Yamaguchi|WD=Q4803120|lt=Asa District|1=Asa District, Yamaguchi}} == References == * {{Cite book|和書|last=Ōta|first=Ryō|date=1934|title=Seishi Kakei Daijiten|publisher=Seishi Kakei Daijiten Kankōkai|url={{NDLDC|1130938/225}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|last=Fujii|first=Takashi|date=2013|title=Muromachi Period Daimyō Power Structures|publisher=Dōseisha}} {{translated page|ja|厚東氏|version=101563902|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:厚東氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11409278}} [[Category:Clans of Chūgoku Region]] [[Category:History of Ube, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Koto Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
